insight: For P1, the opening DD/DC/DDC is considered suboptimal and too slow even for Zemora sets; liadahlia states it is the only reason she would ever consider it, while extratricky notes it is actually the normal P1 Zemora opening unless there is really good absorb.
author: liadahlia, extratricky
context: Discussion about P1 drone-cutting openings and Zemora set strategy.

insight: The only P1 opening that cuts drones on turn 2 considered viable is the 'Elyot Animus' build (which involves a Doomed Drone variant), and even that is described as super niche by liadahlia.
author: liadahlia
context: Discussion about when it is acceptable for P1 to cut drones on turn 2.

insight: Master Bot is only reliably good at acquiring early blue units; sets that reward early blue tech (e.g., Ebb, Flame Animus) are cited as reasons to use Master Bot as a training opponent.
author: liadahlia
context: Discussion of when Master Bot is an appropriate opponent choice based on set composition.

insight: The P1 third-Engineer opening (DD DDE) is underused according to multiple experts; steel0229e and extratricky both advocate for it more frequently, particularly in sets with big absorb units, though it is likely too slow when Blastforge is urgently needed.
author: steel0229e, extratricky
context: Extended debate over whether P1 should go third Engineer in a specific set featuring Infusion Grid, Nivo, and Redeemer.

insight: P1 Natural Blastforge is described by bky as a 'bland compromise opening used to react,' most useful when units like Ebb or Militia allow continued non-commitment; it is generally not considered proactively strong.
author: .bky_1556
context: Discussion of P1 opening options in the context of a set with Tantalum Ray pressure.

insight: In sets with Grimbotch, the correct attacker mix is Grimbotch plus Tarsier rather than Grimbotch alone; double Grimbotch turns are reserved for when it is particularly inconvenient for the opponent to defend (e.g., forcing a drone block or soaking an unclicked Rhino), or in late-game situations when you need burst.
author: .bky_1556
context: Player asking about Grimbotch and Amporilla purchasing decisions in various set compositions.

insight: crash_overlord states as a general rule that if Grimbotch is in the set, Amporilla is never the right buy; while acknowledged as a general heuristic that holds in most cases, exceptions exist (e.g., sets with Xaetron or Fuge that independently enable Amporilla).
author: crash_overlord
context: General discussion about Amporilla vs. Grimbotch purchasing decisions.

insight: Corpus (the red unit that constructs Husks) shifts the Grimbotch/Tarsier mix toward Tarsier, but bky is uncertain whether this is sufficient to make Amporilla viable against tight play.
author: .bky_1556
context: Discussion of Grimbotch, Amporilla, and Corpus interactions in a specific set.

insight: Cauterizer combined with Antima Comet is described as dominant and nearly uncounterable in non-mirror situations; the main timing risk is needing to buy Antima Comet early to prevent the opponent from getting a large one first.
author: Deleted User
context: Discussion of Endotherm Kit viability in a set containing Cauterizer and Antima Comet.

insight: Endotherm Kit is countered by Antima Comet and Cauterizer because the opponent can skip the slower setup unit and buy the faster unit first; masn6811 generalizes this to say that setting up any two of Endotherm Kit, Antima Comet, and Wincer almost never works for this reason.
author: masn6811, apooche
context: Discussion of Endotherm Kit viability against Cauterizer/Antima Comet.

insight: Wincer is positively synergistic with good absorb (more drones available to power shots), Chrono Filter, Energy Matrix, Centurion, and Grenade Mech; it is negatively affected by good delayed soak. The most strongly enabling units are Chrono Filter, then Energy Matrix and Grenade Mech.
author: .bky_1556, Deleted User, masn6811
context: Discussion of what units and set conditions make Wincer strong or weak.

insight: 'Value Wincer' refers to buying Wincer for the deterrent value of 15 HP of future defense rather than intending to fire it; this is contrasted with 'firing Wincer.' Value Wincer is strongest in low-inflation games (where 5 drones matter a lot) or high-inflation games (where 3 turns delay is acceptable, only firing to breach).
author: Deleted User, masn6811
context: Community discussion distinguishing strategic Wincer use cases.

insight: In a breachproof mirror, economy (e.g., Thorium Dynamo) is better than Venge Cannon; against non-breachproof opponents, more Venge Cannons are preferable. Venge Cannon is considered better than Trinity as a drone sink in breachproof sets.
author: mrguy888, masn6811
context: Discussion of drone allocation between Venge Cannon and other breachproof drone sinks.

insight: Holding (not clicking) a big burst unit like Omega Splitter or Arka is generally correct when the opponent has Wall absorb, because clicking forces you to buy roughly 2 extra Walls yourself while only requiring your opponent to buy 1 extra Wall — a net loss. Holding instead lets you buy attackers sooner.
author: mrguy888, mtanzer
context: mtanzer and mrguy888 discussing the mathematics and tempo cost of clicking burst units when the opponent has Wall absorb.

insight: The concept of 'defensive tempo' describes how holding a burst attacker instead of clicking it allows you to buy attack rather than extra soak; approximately 4 tempo is worth roughly 1 damage, because buying an attacker one turn sooner gives it one extra attack.
author: mtanzer
context: mtanzer explaining defensive tempo mathematically using a constructed game example.

insight: Doomed Drone generally benefits the slower side in a matchup, but this effect is not always large enough to change strategic decisions; specifically, in Savior sets, the Savior player wants to sacrifice most Doomed Drones on defense in the turns before Savior arrives rather than using them for economy.
author: masn6811, .bky_1556, themightyflango
context: Discussion of Doomed Drone's interaction with Savior and which strategic side it favors.

insight: When building a third Engineer as P1 because the set is defensive, the correct response to an opponent's early attacker is to continue the drone plan and absorb the attack on Engineers rather than pivoting to early defense or tech buildings, as the third Engineer investment assumes out-econning an early rush.
author: amalloy, crash_overlord, awaclus
context: New player asking how to respond as P1 with a third Engineer when the opponent rushes an attacker.

insight: Preemptive defense is generally wasteful because defense blocking nothing produces no resources; the main exception is non-prompt defenders like Infusion Grid, which are so cost-efficient compared to Wall that buying them early is correct despite the delay.
author: namington, .bky_1556
context: Explaining to a new player the general principle of attack over preemptive defense.

insight: The old P2 Animus/Gaussite Symbiote opening stopped working after Gaussite Symbiote was repriced back to 8RR.
author: .bky_1556
context: Discussion of P2 openings involving alternate drone units.
